conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you properly assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who's 11, and Blake, who's 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (aged 11) and Blake (aged 15).

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake, I need to ask you a few questions.

First question: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, this affects eligibility for Child Benefit.

Could you please tell me: **Which country are you currently living in?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Living in Spain is relevant to your eligibility.

To help determine if you can claim Child Benefit while living in Spain, I need to ask: **Are you working as a Crown servant (for example, for the UK government, diplomatic service, or armed forces), or are you an EU/EEA national with settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant, and I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that information.

Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

According to the UK government's Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education or training) **and you live in the UK**. 

There are only specific exceptions for people living abroad, such as:
- Living in certain countries while working as a Crown servant
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with specific conditions met

Since you are living in Spain and are not a Crown servant, and you do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake at this time.

**For Alex (aged 11):**
While Alex meets the age requirement (under 16), you do not meet the residency requirement as you are living in Spain and don't qualify for any of the exceptions for claiming from abroad.

**For Blake (aged 15):**
While Blake also meets the age requirement (under 16), the same residency issue applies - you are living in Spain without qualifying for the exceptions that would allow you to claim from abroad.

The core requirement for Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK, unless you fall into specific exceptional categories (such as being a Crown servant or having certain EU Settlement Scheme status), which unfortunately don't apply to your situation.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you return to live in the UK - you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 11 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 11 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 11 years old, which is under 16, so the age requirement is met. However, the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions that allow claims from abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme). The fundamental eligibility requirement is that you must live in the UK. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 15 years old, which is under 16, so the age requirement is met. The fundamental eligibility requirement is that you must live in the UK. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
